title: actor_cache global mutex: lock contention DoS under concurrent requests
description:
actor_cache.rs:18: `entries: Mutex<HashMap<String, CachedActor>>` — this is a Tokio async Mutex, but it is held across the entire cache check including the lock acquisition for write after fetch.

More critically: the cache miss path (lines 55-109) does NOT hold the lock while fetching (the HTTP fetch happens with the lock released — line 40 drops the read guard). However, there is no negative-cache entry and no in-flight deduplication. If N concurrent requests arrive for the same uncached actor_uri simultaneously, all N will:
1. Miss the cache (lock released)
2. All fire concurrent HTTP GETs to actor_uri
3. All compete to write the result back

With 10,000 req/sec (attacker varies actor_uri slightly each time to ensure cache misses), broadside makes 10,000 outbound HTTP requests — amplification factor 1:1 but each request holds a reqwest connection. The 30-second timeout means up to 300,000 concurrent outbound connections in the worst case before they time out.

Even without amplification: the global Mutex serializes all cache hits. Under high concurrency, every inbox request blocks on the same lock, degrading throughput to effectively single-threaded for the cache lookup phase.
close_reason: All pentest fixes: require Signature header, verify Digest, enforce actor-keyId match, Date freshness, body size limit 256KB, webhook key via header, decompression bomb limits, symlink rejection, redirect following disabled, delivery SSRF recheck, backslash escape handling
